stat: Annual turnover threshold below which EU AI Act percentage-of-turnover fines are always the operative (lower) cap rather than the fixed euro amount
method: Recipe A — sterling conversion and break-even calculation of EU AI Act Article 99 penalty thresholds

sources_used.title: EU AI Act Article 99 — Penalties
sources_used.publisher: European Commission EU AI Act Service Desk

sources_used.title: EU AI Act full text (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689)
sources_used.publisher: Official Journal of the European Union

workings: Tier 1 (prohibited AI): fixed cap €35m ÷ 7% = €500m break-even turnover. Tier 2 (high-risk and transparency): €15m ÷ 3% = €500m break-even. Tiers 1 and 2 share the same €500m break-even. EUR/GBP conversion at 0.845 (approximate June 2026 market rate): €500m × 0.845 = £422.5m, rounded conservatively to £420m. Below £420m annual global turnover, the % cap always produces the smaller fine in Tiers 1 and 2. Above £420m, the fixed euro cap is the binding limit. Virtually all UK SMEs and most UK mid-caps are below this threshold.
